analysiskmeans 0.0.0.9000
    • Getting Started with analysiskmeans!
    • Package Installation Guide
    • Package Functions
    • Command Line Interface
    • Acknowledgements

Compute Principal Component Analysis

Source: R/kmeans.R
computepca.Rd

Compute Principal Component Analysis

Usage

computepca(mat_norm)

Arguments

mat_norm

A normalized matrix of the top x genes you want to analyze

Value

Resulting PCA matrix

Examples

library(analysiskmeans)
data(example_sce)
SingleCellExperiment::logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(SingleCellExperiment::counts(example_sce))#Line taken from Claude AI - Debug
sce <- example_sce
results <- data_config(sce)
#> Warning: 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated.
#> Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead.
#> See help("Deprecated")
#> Warning: 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated.
#> Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead.
#> See help("Deprecated")
mat_norm <- top_x_genes(results$sce, n_top = 50)
computepca(mat_norm)
#> Standard deviations (1, .., p=20):
#>  [1] 2.517584e+00 2.402654e+00 2.197769e+00 2.060160e+00 1.984220e+00
#>  [6] 1.903726e+00 1.774487e+00 1.634551e+00 1.589758e+00 1.544949e+00
#> [11] 1.371636e+00 1.358595e+00 1.201983e+00 1.136709e+00 1.059692e+00
#> [16] 9.664709e-01 9.002681e-01 8.348507e-01 7.000046e-01 6.064873e-16
#> 
#> Rotation (n x k) = (50 x 20):
#>                PC1          PC2          PC3         PC4          PC5
#>  [1,]  0.042888212  0.188495628 -0.175721034  0.02188276 -0.047606108
#>  [2,] -0.064647519  0.266123616 -0.051433111 -0.05449187 -0.025737638
